business_description.nature_of_business: Commercial construction contractor operating corporate offices, equipment yard and warehouse, fabrication and manufacturing shop, branch offices, and equipment storage facilities across Pennsylvania

coverages_discussed.notes: Derived from loss summary: 35 claims; $1,126,509 total paid; $1,171,607 total incurred. Dominant loss line by frequency and severity.

positives: All locations utilize non-combustible or fire resistive construction types (Fire Resistive, Non-Combustible, Masonry Non-Combustible)
Experienced driver workforce: 15 drivers with experience ranging from 5 to 28 years; average approximately 15.5 years
Total assets of $103,336,700 exceed total liabilities of $68,963,500
Property loss history is minimal: 1 claim, $36,750 total incurred, fully closed
Five-year insurance history demonstrates continuity of coverage with nationally recognized carriers
Favorable loss year in 2022: only 4 claims at $1,369,245 premium
concerns: LOC-005 (Equipment Storage - Cranberry Township, 28,000 sq ft) has no sprinkler system installed
Workers Compensation is a significant loss driver: 35 claims with $1,171,607 total incurred — highest frequency and severity of all lines
Elevated claim counts in 2024 (13 claims) and 2021 (15 claims) per five-year insurance history
GL claims carry substantial open development: $105,421 paid vs. $176,947 incurred ($71,526 in open reserves)
Auto claims also carry open development: $47,076 paid vs. $99,900 incurred ($52,824 in open reserves)
Program premium has trended upward from $1,369,245 (2022) to $1,634,721 (2024), a 19.4% increase over two years
Total liabilities ($68,963,500) exceed equity ($34,373,200), reflecting a leveraged balance sheet (debt-to-equity ratio approximately 2.0x)
Carrier changes across all five years may indicate market difficulty or coverage disputes
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Five-year insurance history (2020–2024) reflects combined program premiums ranging from $1,369,245 (2022 low) to $1,634,721 (2024 high) with annual claim counts ranging from 4 (2022) to 15 (2021). Loss summary across all lines of coverage: Workers Compensation — 35 claims, $1,126,509 paid, $1,171,607 incurred; General Liability — 8 claims, $105,421 paid, $176,947 incurred; Commercial Auto — 8 claims, $47,076 paid, $99,900 incurred; Property — 1 claim, $36,750 paid, $36,750 incurred. Combined total across all lines: 52 claims, $1,315,756 paid, $1,485,204 incurred.

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: Keystone Commercial Builders, Inc. is a commercial construction contractor headquartered at 300 Liberty Avenue, Suite 1200, Pittsburgh, PA 15222. The company operates 5 Pennsylvania locations with a combined total insured value of $39,405,000 spanning office, warehouse, manufacturing, and equipment storage uses. Annual revenue is $95,000,000; total assets are $103,336,700; net income is $5,997,400; equity is $34,373,200. The 2024 insurance program is placed with Nationwide Insurance at a combined premium of $1,634,721.63. An additional named insured is listed as Keystone Commercial Builders, Inc. dba Operations.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: LOC-001: Corporate Headquarters — 18,000 sq ft, 1-story, Fire Resistive, Office occupancy, built 2002, sprinklered, EPDM roof, Pittsburgh PA 15222, TIV $3,600,000. LOC-002: Main Equipment Yard and Warehouse — 65,000 sq ft, 1-story, Non-Combustible, Warehouse occupancy, built 1995, sprinklered, EPDM roof, Pittsburgh PA 15201, TIV $17,550,000. LOC-003: Fabrication Shop — 32,000 sq ft, 1-story, Masonry Non-Combustible, Manufacturing occupancy, built 1988, sprinklered, TPO roof, Pittsburgh PA 15201, TIV $8,000,000. LOC-004: South Hills Branch Office — 8,500 sq ft, 2-story, Masonry Non-Combustible, Office occupancy, built 2010, sprinklered, Shingle roof, Wexford PA 15090, TIV $2,975,000. LOC-005: Equipment Storage Cranberry — 28,000 sq ft, 1-story, Non-Combustible, Warehouse occupancy, built 2005, NO sprinkler, Concrete roof, Cranberry Township PA 16066, TIV $7,280,000. Fleet includes 15 scheduled drivers holding PA licenses with experience ranging from 5 (Kevin Zielinski) to 28 years (Richard Jankowski).
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: Loss summary data reflects cumulative claims across four coverage lines. Workers Compensation is the dominant line with 35 claims and $1,171,607 total incurred ($1,126,509 paid), representing 78.9% of total incurred losses. General Liability shows 8 claims with $176,947 incurred but only $105,421 paid, indicating significant open reserves of $71,526. Commercial Auto reflects 8 claims with $99,900 incurred and $47,076 paid, leaving $52,824 in open development. Property is the most favorable line with a single claim of $36,750 fully paid and closed. Five-year insurance history shows premium and frequency volatility: 2021 produced 15 claims at $1,487,006; 2022 was a favorable year with 4 claims at $1,369,245; 2024 returned to elevated frequency with 13 claims at $1,634,721. Carriers used over the five-year period include Nationwide Insurance (2021, 2024), Berkshire Hathaway Guard (2023), Zurich Insurance Group (2022), and CNA Financial (2020).
